zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Hancock於1480年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼時，她的父親Sir Anthony Hancock20歲，母親Lady Joan Holbach Eaglesfield26歲。她於1504年與John Ward在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1565年在她的家鄉享年86歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Hancock于1480年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼时，她的父亲Sir Anthony Hancock20岁，母亲Lady Joan Holbach Eaglesfield26岁。她于1504年与John Ward在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1565年在她的家乡享年86岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hancock
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Hann + the hypocoristic suffix -cok, which was commonly added to personal names (see Cocke ).
Dutch: from Middle Dutch hanecoc ‘winkle, periwinkle’ (a type of shellfish), probably a metonymic occupational name for someone who gathered and sold shellfish.

History: Thomas Hancock, the uncle of Declaration of Independence signatory John Hancock (1736/7–93), was among the foremost of 18th-century American businessmen. He was a descendant of Nathaniel Hancock, who was known to have been in Cambridge, MA, as early as 1634. Born in Braintree, MA, John Hancock was president of the Second Continental Congress and the first governor of the state of MA.

variants.name: Hancox
meanings: English: variant of Hancock , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Handcock
meanings: English: variant of Hancock with intrusive -d-.

variants.name: Hann
meanings: German and Dutch: from a shortened pet form of the personal name Johann (see John ).

English: from the Middle English personal name Hann, commonly a pet form of Henry (Middle English Hanry). Hann could also be a rhyming pet form of Randolph (Middle English Randal, shortened to Ran or Rand). Han was also a pet form of Joh(a)n in Middle Dutch, and it is possible that this usage was brought to England by Flemings after the Norman Conquest, but no evidence has yet been found that it was similarly used in Middle English. See Henry and Randall , and compare Hankin , Hancock , and Hanks .
English: variant of Ann with prosthetic H-.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Hancock was born in 1480, in Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, England, her father, Sir Anthony Hancock, was 20 and her mother, Lady Joan Holbach Eaglesfield, was 26. She married John Ward in 1504, in Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died about 1565, in her hometown, at the age of 86.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Hancock est née en 1480, à Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Anthony Hancock, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Lady Joan Holbach Eaglesfield, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé John Ward en 1504, à Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée environ 1565, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 86 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Hancock 1480 in Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Anthony Hancock 20 und ihre Mutter Lady Joan Holbach Eaglesfield 26. Sie hat 1504 in Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom John Ward geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb um 1565 im Alter von 86 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Hancock è nata il nel 1480 nel Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, England, sua padre, Sir Anthony Hancock, aveva 20 anni e sua madre, Lady Joan Holbach Eaglesfield, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato John Ward nel 1504, nel Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì circa 1565, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 86 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hancockが1480年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアミッドレトン チェンエイで生まれた時，父Sir Anthony Hancockは20歳，母Lady Joan Holbach Eaglesfieldは26歳であった。1504年にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアミッドレトン チェンエイでJohn Wardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1565年に故郷で86歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hancock(이)가 1480년 영국 노르섬프토실르 미들레턴 천이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Anthony Hancock(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Joan Holbach Eaglesfield(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1504년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 미들레턴 천이에서 John Ward(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1565년 그녀의 고향에서 86세의 나이로 사망했다.
